what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG icon that draws a house joined to a building using currentColor stroked outline paths.
observations: Root svg uses viewBox="0 0 24 24" with no explicit width or height.
Rendering is line-based: fill="none", stroke="currentColor", stroke-width="1.5".
Stroke caps and joins are rounded.
The image is composed of four path elements: a house outline, a small doorway, a right-side building block, and vertical detail marks on the building.
standouts: Uses currentColor so the icon inherits text or CSS color.
Combines residential house and building forms in one compact icon.
The SVG is self-contained and has no external references.
conventions: Follows common outline icon conventions with a 24x24 viewBox, no fill, rounded stroke styling, and currentColor stroke.
Uses inline path data rather than symbol references or grouped sub-elements.
Omits title, desc, class, id, and accessibility attributes.
concerns: No embedded title or desc is present for standalone accessibility semantics.
notes: A compact outline SVG for a combined house-and-building icon.
questions: Should this icon be decorative only, or should consumers provide an accessible label externally?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a building-house icon, and the SVG content depicts a house attached to a building.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable, color-inheriting icon representing a house and adjacent building.
contribution: Contributes a simple themeable outline asset for representing combined residential and building concepts in a fused design system.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor outline SVG icon depicting a house attached to a building with rounded stroked paths.
intention_for_embedding: This was for a reusable design-system icon representing a combined house and building concept.
